chunks.text: A Latinx Heritage Month profile of Dan Garcia - EECS at Berkeley CS alumnus and Teaching Prof. Dan Garcia (M.S. ‘ 95, Ph.D. ’00, advisor: Brian Barsky) is the subject of a profile celebrating Latinx Heritage Month in the EECS department. As a Nuyorican whose father was from Puerto Rico, Garcia “always coveted” his Hispanic heritage and actively explored as much of the Latinx culture as he could. Known to “bring the flavor” to his students before finals, Garcia is particularly passionate about broadening the participation of underrepresented students in computing. Categories Alumni Diversity Faculty View Open Faculty Positions
